what: Create many assignments all at once
title: cnvs: avoiding point and click hell
text: Problem: ten homeworks due every Friday want students submit online. Canvas API docs: https://canvas.instructure.com/doc/api/assignments.html#method.assignments_api.create Start generating tibble hold parameters homeworks, careful dates ISO 8601 format canvas expects: Creating single homework assignment cnvs look like: create , leverage purrr::map2 Delete want:
code: iso8601 <- lubridate::stamp("2019-10-21T18:48:00Z")  hws <- tibble(   week = 1:10,   name = paste("Homework", week),   due = (lubridate::ymd_hm("2026-09-27 23:59", tz = "America/Los_Angeles") +     lubridate::weeks(week - 1)) %>% iso8601() ) hws one_hw <-   cnvs("POST /api/v1/courses/:course_id/assignments",     course_id = test_course,     assignment = list(       name = hws$name[[1]],       due_at = hws$due[[1]],       points_possible = 10,       submission_types = list("online_upload")     ) ) all_hws <- map2(hws$name, hws$due,   ~ cnvs("POST /api/v1/courses/:course_id/assignments",       course_id = test_course,       assignment = list(         name = .x,         due_at = .y,         points_possible = 10,         submission_types = list("online_upload")       )   ) ) all_hws %>%   map_int("id") %>%   walk(~ cnvs("DELETE /api/v1/courses/:course_id/assignments/:id",     id = .,     course_id = test_course))

title: Getting started with cnvs
text: use API Canvas need access token. Access tokens specific user account Canvas domain. Canvas domain, CANVAS_DOMAIN, URL institution’s Canvas instance, e.g. https://oregonstate.instructure.com , instance provided Instructure https://canvas.instructure.com/. can request access token : {CANVAS_DOMAIN}/profile/settings, “Approved Integrations:”. generated, token visible make sure copy . verify token domain, pass .token .api_url arguments cnvs_whoami(): result successful include name login id: convenient set environment variables store domain token. cnvs looks CANVAS_DOMAIN CANVAS_API_TOKEN respectively. easiest way set edit .Renviron file: Add lines like substituting domain token: Make sure .Renviron file ends empty line. Restart R check running cnvs_whoami() arguments:
code: cnvs_whoami(.token = "mvvGbKyGK9n5T57qhEu8K1sNMt85OLoNGTepqd3v5NEcWMuxArSz5aaXppPjodr5eU",            .api_url = "https://canvas.instructure.com") "name": "Charlotte Wickham",   "login_id": "cwickham@gmail.com",   "domain": "https://canvas.instructure.com",   "token": "mv..." # install.packages("usethis") usethis::edit_r_environ() CANVAS_DOMAIN="https://canvas.instructure.com" CANVAS_API_TOKEN="mvvGbKyGK9n5T57qhEu8K1sNMt85OLoNGTepqd3v5NEcWMuxArSz5aaXppPjodr5eU" cnvs_whoami() "name": "Charlotte Wickham",   "login_id": "cwickham@gmail.com",   "domain": "https://canvas.instructure.com",   "token": "mv..."

title: Getting started with cnvs
text: default cnvs() endpoint /api/v1/courses lists courses: can also find course ID visiting course Canvas examining URL: course ID comes right /courses/, e.g. 1732420 case. examples , ’ll use course_id one courses — ’ll need substitute :
code: my_courses <- cnvs() https://canvas.instructure.com/courses/1732420 course_id <- 14337283

title: Getting started with cnvs
text: make query Canvas LMS API use cnvs() function. first argument API endpoint. cnvs() designed make easy possible copy paste Canvas API documentation (also available developerdocs.instructure.com). example, imagine want see discussion topics course. first step find task Canvas API docs — listed Discussions resource List discussion topics. two endpoints listed : GET /api/v1/courses/:course_id/discussion_topics GET /api/v1/groups/:group_id/discussion_topics first list topics course second group — want first. Parts endpoint prefaced colon, :, parameters, e.g. :course_id :group_id. need provide parameters cnvs() named arguments (minus :). make query, copy paste endpoint first argument cnvs(), add arguments parameters endpoint:
code: discussions <- cnvs("GET /api/v1/courses/:course_id/discussion_topics",   course_id = course_id)

title: Getting started with cnvs
text: cnvs() returns list, prints list JSON. can access components elements list: Functions parsing results beyond scope cnvs, can parse using iteration functions purrr. example, look topic titles: squeeze entire response tibble:
code: discussions[[1]]$title library(purrr) library(dplyr) discussions |>   map_chr("title") discussions |>   map(\(x) {     x |>       compact() |> # remove NULLs       map_if(is.list, list) |> # push nested lists down one level       as_tibble()   }) |>   list_rbind()

title: Getting started with cnvs
text: Canvas API docs also describe parameters can passed body query endpoint “Parameters” section. can add additional named arguments cnvs(). example, List discussion topics possible parameter only_announcements, boolean, controls whether announcements returned. add call cnvs() another argument: parameters expect complicated object, example parameters create module, include: module[name] module[unlock_at] module[position] notation, parameter name followed another square brackets, indicates hierarchical structure. Canvas expecting module parameter JSON object. cnvs() translates named list, e.g.: square brackets empty, Canvas expecting JSON array, translates unnamed list cnvs(). example structure quiz_extensions parameter set quiz extensions, e.g. quiz_extensions[][user_id] quiz_extensions[][extra_attempts]
code: announcements <- cnvs("GET /api/v1/courses/:course_id/discussion_topics",   course_id = course_id,   only_announcements = TRUE) announcements |>   map_chr("title") new_module <- cnvs("POST /api/v1/courses/:course_id/modules",   course_id = course_id,   module = list(     name = "First module",     unlock_at = "2019-09-01T6:59:00Z",     position = 1   )) quiz_ext <- cnvs("POST /api/v1/courses/:course_id/quizzes/:quiz_id/extensions",   course_id = course_id,   quiz_id = 62851823,   quiz_extensions = list(     list(       user_id = 99999,       extra_attempts = 1     ),     list(       user_id = 99998,       extra_attempts = 2     )   ) )

title: Getting started with cnvs
text: upload files use cnvs_upload() function. need locate endpoint required context file. E.g. upload course file endpoint : Whereas upload file submission endpoint : Pass path file wish upload cnvs_upload(): default endpoint uploads course. Like cnvs() can specify endpoint parameters like course_id, request body parameters like parent_folder_path, additional arguments.
code: POST /api/v1/courses/:course_id/files POST /api/v1/courses/:course_id/assignments/:assignment_id/submissions/:user_id/files cnvs_upload("notes.pdf", course_id = course_id, parent_folder_path = "handouts/")

title: Canvas LMS API
text: Minimalistic client access Canvas LMS API. Heavily borrowing infrastructure gh

title: Canvas LMS API
text: cnvs intentionally minimalist. use , need become familiar Canvas API documentation. cnvs checking endpoints provide, objects pass. advantage cnvs dependent specifics Canvas API. cnvs also parsing response content, need extract desired information returned lists . cnvs facilitate automation repetitive tasks Canvas R, still requires fair bit expertise user. hope use foundation higher level api package, user-friendly.

title: Canvas LMS API
text: Install package GitHub:
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("cwickham/cnvs")

title: Canvas LMS API
text: Use cnvs() function access API endpoints. endpoints listed documentation. first argument cnvs() endpoint. Note leading /api/v1/ must included well, facilitates copy pasting directly documentation. Parameters can passed extra arguments. E.g. JSON result sent API converted R object. end point parameters, can also passed extra arguments:
code: library(cnvs) my_courses <- cnvs("/api/v1/courses", enrollment_type = "teacher") vapply(my_courses, "[[", "", "name") [1] "Test Course" test_modules <- cnvs("/api/v1/courses/:course_id/modules",   course_id = 14337283) vapply(test_modules, "[[", "", "name") [1] "First module" "First module" "First module" "First module" "First module"

what: POST, PATCH, PUT and DELETE requests
title: Canvas LMS API
text: POST, PUT, DELETE requests can sent including HTTP verb endpoint, first argument. example, create module: update name module: , finally, delete module:
code: new_module <- cnvs("POST /api/v1/courses/:course_id/modules",   course_id = 14337283,  # set a parameter in the endpoint `:course_id`   module = list(        # a parameter sent in the body     name = "First module",     position = 1   ) ) test_modules <- cnvs("/api/v1/courses/:course_id/modules",   course_id = 14337283) vapply(test_modules, "[[", "", "name") [1] "First module" "First module" "First module" "First module" "First module" [6] "First module" update_module <- cnvs("PUT /api/v1/courses/:course_id/modules/:id",   course_id = 14337283,   id = new_module$id,   module = list(     name = "Module 1"   ) ) test_modules <- cnvs("/api/v1/courses/:course_id/modules",   course_id = 14337283) vapply(test_modules, "[[", "", "name") [1] "Module 1"     "First module" "First module" "First module" "First module" [6] "First module" cnvs("DELETE /api/v1/courses/:course_id/modules/:id",   course_id = 14337283,   id = new_module$id ) {   "id": 22583369,   "position": 1,   "name": "Module 1",   "unlock_at": {},   "require_sequential_progress": false,   "requirement_type": "all",   "publish_final_grade": false,   "prerequisite_module_ids": [],   "published": false,   "items_count": 0,   "items_url": "https://canvas.instructure.com/api/v1/courses/14337283/modules/22583369/items" } test_modules <- cnvs("/api/v1/courses/:course_id/modules",   course_id = 14337283) vapply(test_modules, "[[", "", "name") [1] "First module" "First module" "First module" "First module" "First module"

title: Canvas LMS API
text: Supply page parameter get subsequent pages:
code: my_courses2 <- cnvs("/api/v1/courses", enrollment_type = "teacher",   page = 2) vapply(my_courses2, "[[", "", "name")

what: Query the Canvas LMS API — cnvs
title: Query the Canvas LMS API — cnvs
text: extremely minimal client. need know API able use client. function : Try substitute listed parameter endpoint, using {parameter} :parameter notation. GET request (default), add listed parameters query parameters. GET request, send parameters request body, JSON. Convert response R list using jsonlite::fromJSON().

title: Query the Canvas LMS API — cnvs
code: cnvs(   endpoint = "/api/v1/courses",   ...,   per_page = NULL,   .per_page = NULL,   .token = NULL,   .destfile = NULL,   .overwrite = FALSE,   .api_url = NULL,   .method = "GET",   .limit = NULL,   .accept = "application/json",   .send_headers = NULL,   .progress = TRUE,   .params = list(),   .max_wait = 600,   .max_rate = NULL )

title: Query the Canvas LMS API — cnvs
text: endpoint Canvas API endpoint. Defaults /api/v1/courses lists courses. Must one following forms: METHOD path, e.g. GET /api/v1/courses, path, e.g. /api/v1/courses, METHOD url, e.g. GET https://canvas.instructure.com/api/v1/courses, url, e.g. https://canvas.instructure.com/api/v1/courses. method supplied, use .method, defaults "GET". ... Name-value pairs giving API parameters. matched endpoint placeholders, sent query parameters GET requests, JSON body POST requests. one unnamed parameter, raw vector, JSON encoded, sent raw data, . can used example add assets releases. Named NULL values silently dropped. GET requests, named NA values trigger error. methods, named NA values included body request, JSON null. per_page, .per_page Number items return per page. omitted, substituted max(.limit, 100) .limit set, otherwise determined API (never greater 100). .token Authentication token. Defaults cnvs_token(). .destfile Path write response disk. NULL (default), response processed returned object. path given, response written disk form sent. cnvs writes response temporary file, renames file .destfile request successful. name temporary file created adding -<random>.cnvs-tmp suffix , <random> ASCII string random characters. cnvs removes temporary file error. .overwrite .destfile provided, whether overwrite existing file.  Defaults FALSE. error happens original file kept. .api_url Canvas domain URL. Used endpoint just contains path. Defaults CANVAS_DOMAIN environment variable set. .method HTTP method use explicitly supplied endpoint. .limit Number records return. can used instead manual pagination. default NULL, means defaults Canvas API used. can set number request (less) records, also Inf request records. Note, request many records, multiple Canvas API calls used get , can take potentially long time. .accept value Accept HTTP header. Defaults "application/json". Accept given .send_headers, used. .send_headers Named character vector header field values (except Authorization, handled via .token). can used override augment default User-Agent header: "https://github.com/cwickham/cnvs". .progress Whether show progress indicator calls need one HTTP request. .params Additional list parameters append .... easier use ... parameters list already. .max_wait Maximum number seconds wait rate limited. Defaults 10 minutes. .max_rate Maximum request rate requests per second. Set automatically throttle requests.

title: Query the Canvas LMS API — cnvs
text: Answer API cnvs_response object, also list. Failed requests generate R error. Requests generate raw response return raw vector.

title: Query the Canvas LMS API — cnvs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## List your courses cnvs("/api/v1/courses")  ## Get a specific course cnvs("/api/v1/courses/{course_id}", course_id = 123456)  ## Same thing with :param syntax cnvs("/api/v1/courses/:course_id", course_id = 123456)  ## List assignments for a course cnvs("/api/v1/courses/{course_id}/assignments", course_id = 123456)  ## Create an assignment cnvs(   "POST /api/v1/courses/{course_id}/assignments",   course_id = 123456,   assignment = list(     name = "Homework 1",     points_possible = 10   ) )  ## Automatic pagination - get all students students <- cnvs(   "/api/v1/courses/{course_id}/users",   course_id = 123456,   enrollment_type = "student",   .limit = Inf ) } # }

what: Get the next, previous, first or last page of results — cnvs_next
title: Get the next, previous, first or last page of results — cnvs_next
text: Get next, previous, first last page results

title: Get the next, previous, first or last page of results — cnvs_next
code: cnvs_next(cnvs_response, .token = NULL, .send_headers = NULL)  cnvs_prev(cnvs_response, .token = NULL, .send_headers = NULL)  cnvs_first(cnvs_response, .token = NULL, .send_headers = NULL)  cnvs_last(cnvs_response, .token = NULL, .send_headers = NULL)

title: Get the next, previous, first or last page of results — cnvs_next
text: cnvs_response object returned cnvs() call. .token Authentication token. Defaults cnvs_token(). .send_headers Named character vector header field values (except Authorization, handled via .token). can used override augment default User-Agent header: "https://github.com/cwickham/cnvs".

title: Get the next, previous, first or last page of results — cnvs_next
text: Answer API.

title: Get the next, previous, first or last page of results — cnvs_next
text: Note always defined. E.g. first page queried (default), first previous pages defined. next page, next page defined, etc. requested page exist, error thrown.

title: Get the next, previous, first or last page of results — cnvs_next
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ x <- cnvs("/api/v1/courses") x2 <- cnvs_next(x) } # }

what: Return the local user's Canvas token and domain — cnvs_token
title: Return the local user's Canvas token and domain — cnvs_token
text: cnvs_token() returns Canvas API token CANVAS_API_TOKEN environment variable. Canvas API requests require token prove request authorized specific Canvas user. cnvs_domain() returns Canvas domain URL CANVAS_DOMAIN environment variable. institution's Canvas URL, e.g. https://canvas.instructure.com https://myuni.instructure.com. can generate token Canvas profile settings page {CANVAS_DOMAIN}/profile/settings "Approved Integrations". See cnvs_whoami() details setting credentials.

title: Return the local user's Canvas token and domain — cnvs_token
code: cnvs_token()  cnvs_token_exists()  cnvs_domain()

title: Return the local user's Canvas token and domain — cnvs_token
text: string. cnvs_token(), return value S3 class ensure simple printing strategies reveal entire token. functions error corresponding environment variable set.

title: Return the local user's Canvas token and domain — cnvs_token
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cnvs_token() cnvs_domain()  format(cnvs_token()) } # }

title: Upload files to Canvas — cnvs_upload
text: Upload local file location Canvas LMS. Read possible parameters https://canvas.instructure.com/doc/api/file.file_uploads.html.

title: Upload files to Canvas — cnvs_upload
code: cnvs_upload(path, endpoint = "/api/v1/courses/:course_id/files", ...)

title: Upload files to Canvas — cnvs_upload
text: path Path file upload endpoint Character string API endpoint upload. depends required context file. example, default, "/api/v1/courses/:course_id/files" uploads file course. Uploading file assignment submission endpoint "/api/v1/courses/:course_id/assignments/:assignment_id/submissions/:user_id/files" ... parameters passed along cnvs(). Use specify endpoint parameters, e.g. course_id, parameters file upload, e.g. parent_folder_path, parameters cnvs() like .token .api_url.

title: Upload files to Canvas — cnvs_upload
text: Invisibly returns response upload confirmation. success, prints download URL uploaded file.

title: Upload files to Canvas — cnvs_upload
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## Upload a course file, ends up in "unfiled" cnvs_upload("notes.pdf", course_id = "1732420")  ## Upload a course file to a specific folder cnvs_upload("notes.pdf",   course_id = "1732420", parent_folder_path = "cnvs_files") } # }

what: Info on current Canvas user, domain and token — cnvs_whoami
title: Info on current Canvas user, domain and token — cnvs_whoami
text: Reports name Canvas login ID current authenticated user, Canvas domain first bit token.

title: Info on current Canvas user, domain and token — cnvs_whoami
code: cnvs_whoami(.token = NULL, .api_url = NULL, .send_headers = NULL)

title: Info on current Canvas user, domain and token — cnvs_whoami
text: .token Authentication token. Defaults cnvs_token(). .api_url Canvas domain URL. Used endpoint just contains path. Defaults CANVAS_DOMAIN environment variable set. .send_headers Named character vector header field values (except Authorization, handled via .token). can used override augment default User-Agent header: "https://github.com/cwickham/cnvs".

title: Info on current Canvas user, domain and token — cnvs_whoami
text: cnvs_response object, also list.

title: Info on current Canvas user, domain and token — cnvs_whoami
text: Canvas domain address organization's Canvas site, e.g. https://oregonstate.instructure.com/. Set via CANVAS_DOMAIN environment variable pass .api_url. Get personal access token Canvas domain {CANVAS_DOMAIN}/profile/settings. token string letters digits. can store way like provide explicitly via .token argument cnvs(). However, many prefer define environment variables CANVAS_API_TOKEN CANVAS_DOMAIN values .Renviron file. Add lines look like , substituting domain token:   Put line break end! using editor shows line numbers, (least) three lines, third one empty. Restart R take effect. Call cnvs_whoami() confirm success. get complete information authenticated user, call cnvs("/api/v1/users/self").
code: CANVAS_DOMAIN="https://canvas.instructure.com" CANVAS_API_TOKEN="your_token_here"

title: Info on current Canvas user, domain and token — cnvs_whoami
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cnvs_whoami()  ## explicit token + domain cnvs_whoami(   .token = "your_token_here",   .api_url = "https://canvas.instructure.com" ) } # }
